stories.headline: US releases video of forces seizing Iranian ship
stories.summary: The US military released footage of forces seizing an Iranian ship, marking an escalation in tensions over control of the strategic Strait of Hormuz. The seizure has triggered concerns about regional stability and prompted oil price increases as markets react to potential disruptions in global shipping routes.

stories.headline: Magnitude 7.5 earthquake strikes northern Japan; tsunami warning issued
stories.summary: A magnitude 7.5 earthquake struck off Japan's northern coast, prompting authorities to issue a tsunami warning for coastal areas in the region. The powerful seismic event creates potential risk for communities along the affected shoreline, though the full extent of damage and tsunami impact remains to be assessed.

stories.headline: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
stories.summary: A man shot and killed eight children in Louisiana, seven of whom were his own, in a mass shooting that also left other people wounded. Authorities are investigating the circumstances that led to the attack at the residence.

stories.headline: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
stories.summary: Rumen Radev's party secures a decisive majority in Bulgaria's election, giving the former president a strong mandate to form a government after years of political instability. His victory reflects voter frustration with fragmented coalitions and deadlock, though his foreign policy alignment with Russia distinguishes his platform from previous administrations.

stories.headline: Two arrested over Kenton synagogue attack
stories.summary: Two teenagers have been arrested following an attempted arson attack at a synagogue in Kenton, north-west London. British police are investigating potential connections to Iranian regime proxies as part of a broader pattern of antisemitic arson attacks targeting Jewish sites in the capital.

stories.headline: Ukraine claims attack on Russian warships in occupied Crimea
stories.summary: Ukrainian forces report striking Russian naval vessels stationed in Crimea, continuing efforts to target military infrastructure in the occupied territory. The claim reflects ongoing attacks across multiple fronts, including reported drone operations in southern Russia and strikes on Black Sea port facilities.

stories.headline: Humanoid robots show rapid advances as they race past humans in Beijing half marathon
stories.summary: Humanoid robots completed a half-marathon in Beijing, finishing faster than human participants in the same event. The achievement demonstrates significant progress in robotics engineering, particularly in endurance, locomotion, and real-world performance capabilities outside controlled laboratory settings.

stories.headline: US strike on alleged drug boat in Caribbean kills 3
stories.summary: A U.S. military strike targeting an alleged drug trafficking vessel in the Caribbean results in three deaths. The operation reflects ongoing American counternarcotics efforts in the region, though details about the boat's cargo and the circumstances of the strike remain limited.

stories.headline: Outrage after Israeli soldier desecrates statue of Jesus Christ in Lebanon
stories.summary: An Israeli soldier damaged a statue of Jesus Christ in southern Lebanon, an act captured in photographs that spread widely online. Israel's military confirmed the image's authenticity and opened an investigation into the incident, while officials condemned the soldier's actions.

stories.headline: ‘We’re Catholic first’: Sunday mass attenders weigh in on Trump’s feud with Pope Leo
stories.summary: Pope Leo has criticized Donald Trump on matters of policy or conduct, creating tension that resonates among Catholic congregants in the United States. American Catholics attending mass express a priority to their faith identity over political allegiances when navigating this public disagreement between their church's leadership and a prominent political figure.
